bug_description: The inner digit-scan loop condition uses only `expr[j].isdigit()` instead of `expr[j].isdigit() or expr[j] == '.'`. Decimal numbers (e.g. '3.14') are split at the decimal point: the integer part is emitted as NUM, the '.' is treated as an unknown character and silently skipped (falls through to the final `i += 1`), and the fractional part is emitted as a separate NUM. Multi-part decimal literals are tokenized incorrectly.

reference_fix.replacement: while j < n and (expr[j].isdigit() or expr[j] == "."):
